name: Olorun
description: Olorun is the Supreme God in Yoruba mythology. He is the ruler of the heavens and is often considered the ultimate source of all existence and the creator of the universe. Though he is the supreme being, Olorun is largely detached from the daily affairs of humans. He is considered too powerful and transcendent to be directly approached or worshipped. Instead, people communicate with him through the Orishas, who act as intermediaries between Olorun and humanity. Olorun is the source of Ashe, the divine life force that permeates all things. He is also associated with the sun and the sky, representing his distance and vastness. While not actively involved in the world, his presence is felt through the natural order and the power he bestows upon the Orishas. Olorun is the final judge of all souls, determining their fate after death. He represents ultimate power, creation, and the source of all life, though his aloofness necessitates the worship of the more accessible Orishas for everyday needs and concerns. He is seen as the ultimate authority and the source of justice and order in the cosmos. His power is so immense that direct interaction is impossible, making the Orishas essential for connection with the divine.
subtitle: Supreme God, Creator of the Universe

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: Olorun sent Obatala down to create the earth from the primordial waters. Obatala, after drinking palm wine, made imperfect humans, which is why some have deformities.

keyMyths.title: The Giving of Ashe
keyMyths.description: Olorun is the source of Ashe, the divine life force that empowers all things and allows the Orishas to perform their duties.

keyMyths.title: The Final Judgment
keyMyths.description: After death, Olorun judges the souls of the deceased, determining their fate based on their actions in life.

sources.author: Idowu, E. Bolaji
sources.description: Comprehensive study of Yoruba religious beliefs.
sources.text: Comprehensive study of Yoruba religious beliefs.
